findings.title: Window rollover can lose or misclassify concurrent URMA samples
findings.impact: After this CAS publishes the new window start, another request thread can observe the new window and increment successCount_ or failureCount_ before the rollover thread exchanges those counters to zero on lines 57-58. That new-window sample is then either included in the expired-window snapshot or erased, so concurrent client traffic can produce false worker failovers or miss real URMA data-plane failures.
findings.fix: Make rollover linearizable with the counter reset, for example by protecting windowStartTimeMs_ and the counters with one mutex/spin lock, or by using a generation/double-buffer design where recorders increment the counter for the generation they observed and the rollover swaps generations before reading only the sealed old counters.
